---
title: "Inference Without Data: A Causal Transportability Framework for Ketamine Effects in TGD Populations"
description: "How do you estimate a treatment effect for a population that's never been in a clinical trial? This thesis builds a causal data fusion framework for estimating ketamine's effects on chronic pain and depression in transgender and gender-diverse patients using two published studies that individually can't answer the question. Uses Pearl's transportability theory, a derived anchor constraint, and constrained prior predictive Monte Carlo to characterize transported estimates without access to individual-level data."
author:
  - name: Z Skigen
    affiliation: Pomona College Mathematics and Statistics Department
    affiliation-url: https://www.pomona.edu/academics/departments/mathematics-statistics
date: 2026-04-18
categories: [Causal Inference, Bayesian Inference, Healthcare]
citation:
  url: https://zskigen.github.io/projects/thesis/
image: selectiondiagram.png
draft: true
---

Write about the project here...
